subject: Computer Networking
title: Wi-Fi Generations Explained
description: Learn Wi-Fi Generations Explained in one minute.

hook.text: Wi-Fi 4, 5, 6, 6E, 7 — the numbers keep climbing. Each generation solves problems the last one couldn't.

buildup.text: Wi-Fi 4 brought MIMO. Wi-Fi 5 moved to 5 GHz for speed. Wi-Fi 6 added efficiency for crowded environments.

discovery.text: Wi-Fi 6 introduced OFDMA, letting the router talk to many devices simultaneously instead of one at a time.

twist.text: Wi-Fi 6E opened the 6 GHz band — a huge chunk of clean spectrum. Like adding new lanes to a jammed highway.

climax.text: Wi-Fi 7 will support 320 MHz channels and multi-link operation. Speeds could exceed 40 Gbps in theory.

punchline.text: Each Wi-Fi generation doubles down on speed, capacity, and efficiency.

quiz.question: What did Wi-Fi 6E add compared to Wi-Fi 6?
options: Access to the 6 GHz frequency band
